html:
Runestone, 1.7 m high, 0.8-1.0 m wide, and 0.3 m thick. The height of the runes is 6-12 cm. The runestone faces west with its inscribed surface. Part of the northern rune loop is damaged. It has been photographed. U785. Adjacent to and below runestone 1: A grave marker, made of limestone, 0.4 m high. At the top, there is a depression, 9 cm in diameter and 6 cm thick. An inscription resembling a chain runs around the grave marker, with a width of 3-4 cm. This description has not been quality checked. Information may be missing, inaccurate, or outdated. Please refer to the inventory book for more information.

Location: Sweden, Uppsala, Enköping, Uppland, Tillinge.

Rundata identification number: U 785.

How certain are we about this?

This is the National Heritage Board's archaeological registration of the stone itself – a physical and documented historical monument. The runic inscription itself is a contemporary primary source, but the interpretation of the text and its context may be uncertain, and dating relies on rune type and style rather than specific years.
tldr: This is a description of an ancient runestone located in Tillinge, Uppsala County. The text details the stone's dimensions, appearance, and a nearby grave marker (gravklot).
lattlast:
The text describes a runestone that is almost two meters tall and has been painted to make it more visible. It is located in Tillinge, Uppland, and is registered with the Swedish National Heritage Board (Riksantikvarieämbetet), which means it is protected as a historical monument.

Next to the runestone, there is also a gravestone made of limestone. It is a small stone with an engraved chain around it. Because it is an old stone, some details in the description may be uncertain or missing, but it serves as an important historical source.

The runestone has been assigned a unique number, U 785, to allow it to be easily found in official registers. Although we do not know exactly which year the stone was erected, researchers can analyze its style and type to determine its age.
